retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Teeth -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Amelogenesis imperfecta: It is an inherited dental defect of the enamel characterized by abnormally soft and fragile enamel resulting in thin enamel, which can easily wear off or chip off, exposing the underlying dentin and causing yellow to brown discoloration. It affects both primary and permanent dentition.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Anticariogenic Action
retrieved_docs.content: Various mechanisms can explain the anticariogenic action of fluoride, including decreased demineralization, increased remineralization, enhancing the resistance of enamel to acid attack by conversion of hydroxyapatite to fluorapatite, and the inhibition of enolase enzyme, thus interrupting microbial replication and metabolism. A sustained fluoride release from the restoration in the marginal gaps (between restoration and tooth) helps prevent secondary caries. This property of GIC makes it the material of choice for restorations in patients with high caries activity.

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Treatment / Management -- Enamel Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: The clinician can consider smoothing any sharp edges. No clinical or radiographic follow-up is recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: Epidermolysis Bullosa -- Complications -- Oral and Gastrointestinal Disease
retrieved_docs.content: Repeated blistering, erosion, ulceration, and scarring in the oral mucosa secondary to dysfunctional laminin 332, kindlin-1, and types VII and XVII collagen can cause microstomia, tongue-tethering and vestibular sulcus loss. Junctional epidermolysis bullosa additionally features tooth enamel defect. Combined, these concerns increase the likelihood of dental caries formation due to difficulty maintaining oral hygiene. [74] [75] Management may include dental scaling, root planning, and antibiotic therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Indications for the use of stainless steel crowns: Following pulp therapy Multisurface caries restorations Patients at high risk of caries Deciduous teeth with developmental defects, such as amelogenesis imperfecta, dentinogenesis imperfecta, enamel hypoplasia Teeth with extensive wear Where it is likely that the restoration may fail, eg, the proximal box formed is extended beyond the anatomic line angles. Fractured teeth Abutment for space maintainer [6] Patients who are unlikely to attend follow up appointments [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Ectodermal Dysplasia -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: 4. Ankyloblepharon-Ectodermal Defects-Cleft Lip/Palate Syndrome: Also known as Hay-Wells syndrome. Clinical features are evident right from birth. A classic erythrodermic presentation with peeling skin and underlying erosions is common, resulting in potentially fatal infections due to acute skin failure. The scalp is invariably involved in the form of a chronic oozing erosive dermatitis, patchy alopecia, and wiry hair—hair-shaft abnormalities such as the pili-torti range from hyperconvexity to anonychia. Sweating may be decreased with resultant thermal intolerance. Dental abnormalities include hypodontia and conical teeth. Additional features that differentiate this syndrome from other variants include hyper granulation tissue formation, recurrent skin infections, cribriform and stellate scarring of the shoulders and upper trunk, and reticulated pigmentation of the intertriginous areas. Congenital strands of tissue, also known as ankyloblepharon adnatum filiform, are observed between the eyelids, which might require surgical correction or resolve spontaneously. Lacrimal duct atresia may occur. External ear malformation may be observed. Almost all patients of AEC present with a cleft palate with or without cleft lip. Gastroesophageal reflux causing failure to thrive is present in most patients and is severe enough to warrant gastrostomy placement. Hypospadias, supernumerary nipples, and limb abnormalities are other associated features. [10] [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Tooth -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Enamel is a non-renewable tissue, and enamel preservation is crucial for the dental professional. The physical properties and histology must be considered when treating and counseling patients, and appropriate knowledge of enamel sealants, polishing, and other products is pivotal.

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Anatomy and Physiology
retrieved_docs.content: The morphology of the occlusal surface of the molars is extraordinarily variable. Occlusal fissures are deep invaginations of enamel that can be extremely diverse in shape and have been described as broad or narrow funnels, constricted hourglasses, and multiple invaginations with inverted Y-shaped divisions and irregularly shaped. Due to this morphological diversity, categorizing a tooth as having a single, definitive type of fissure is often not possible. [2] Fissure types include: V type: Wide at the top and gradually narrowing towards the bottom; shallow and wide; tends to be self-cleaning. U-type: Shallow and wide; tends to be self-cleaning. I-type: Extremely narrow slit; deep, narrow, and constricted, resembling a bottleneck; more caries susceptible. IK- type: A narrow slit associated with a larger shape at the bottom; may require invasive technique and are very susceptible to caries.

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Epithelial Defect -- Treatment / Management -- Treatment of Refractory Cases
retrieved_docs.content: Patients who do not respond to the modalities discussed above are labeled as refractory cases. Amniotic membrane grafting or transplant (AMT) is effective in refractory cases by providing a scaffold for re-epithelialization, anti-inflammatory properties, and decreasing vascularization and scarring. [1] [25] It contains numerous growth factors, proteinase inhibitors, and proteins that aid in healing. It can be fibrin-glued or sutured beneath a BCL or inserted as a self-retaining device. Post-operative infection, suture breakage, membrane dissolution, and hemorrhage are among the few reported complications.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Amelogenesis imperfecta ranges from mild to severe, which will dictate the extent of the treatment required. As far as management is concerned, treatment should be considered in symptomatic cases or cases where the patient expresses esthetic concerns. Treatment can range from composite fillings and veneers to full-coverage ceramic restorations depending on the severity of the condition.

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- History and Physical -- Enamel Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: These fractures are limited to the enamel only. No radiographic examination is recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: Early Childhood Caries -- Etiology -- Developmental Enamel Defects
retrieved_docs.content: Developmental enamel defects, such as enamel hypoplasia, have been recognized as a significant risk factor for developing ECC [16] - Streptococcus mutans rapidly colonize the rough defects of the enamel surface.

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Enamel Fracture
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment options depend on fragment availability and the extent of the lesion. The tooth fragment may be reattached, the edges may be smoothed, and the defect may be restored with resin composite. Clinical and radiographic follow-up is recommended after 2 months and again at 1 year to assess the restoration and look for signs of pulp necrosis, apical periodontitis, and, in immature teeth, impaired root development.

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Enamel-Dentin Fracture
retrieved_docs.content: If the tooth fragment is available, reattachment should be considered. The fragment must be soaked in water or saline solution for 20 minutes before the procedure to restore tissue hydration. Other treatment options include direct composite restoration, composite restoration with a waxup, and porcelain and resin-based indirect restorations.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: As all of the above conditions are genetic or developmental in origin, there is no true way to deter or prevent these conditions from occurring. Patients that present with generalized developmental defects affecting the entire dentition should be evaluated for an underlying syndrome. Treatment of the above-listed conditions is likely symptomatically or esthetically driven. Patients presenting with these conditions should be encouraged to self-monitor for new or worsening symptoms that dental professionals will evaluate and treat.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Dens invaginatus, also known as dens in dente, is a dental malformation that results from an inward folding of the developing tooth germ. Dental anomalies have a multifactorial etiology, with genetic and environmental factors being the most common. [3] The etiology of this condition is not well understood; however, it is likely related to an additional invagination of the inner enamel epithelium into the interior aspect of the developing tooth bud before mineralization. [4] Several other suggested etiologies range from the rapid proliferation of part of the inner enamel epithelium into the dental papilla to trauma and an absence of signaling molecules for morphogenesis. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 895
retrieved_docs.content: 0.0088 MG/MG Toothpaste. Across these preparations, sodium monofluorophosphate provides fluoride ions that inhibit demineralization and promote remineralization of tooth enamel, thereby reducing the incidence of dental caries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Accidental physical contact (event) is described as being due to loss of teeth caused by an accident, indicating that dental injury resulting from an accident can lead to unintended physical contact.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

Recognizing these relationships aids clinicians in systematically assessing and managing the long‑term sequelae of diverse traumatic injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 1184
retrieved_docs.content: effects as specific conditions associated with the concept of “كسر,” situates them within a broader category of unspecified injury sequelae, and further delineates limb‑specific fracture sequelae, thereby providing a structured framework for understanding and managing the long‑term consequences of various bone injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 4 -- Community 2707
retrieved_docs.content: خديج is clinically associated with cervical insufficiency (قصور عنق الرحم), indicating a direct link between this condition and weakened cervical competence. Additionally, recurrent miscarriage (إجهاض معتاد) is identified as a parent condition of cervical insufficiency, suggesting that women who experience repeated pregnancy loss may have an underlying cervical insufficiency contributing to the miscarriages. Recognizing both the association of خديج with cervical insufficiency and the role of إجهاض معتاد as a precursor condition is important for accurate diagnosis and targeted management of patients at risk for cervical incompetence.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 362
retrieved_docs.content: Other bone disease and musculoskeletal deformities are associated with bone resorption, indicating that these conditions often involve increased breakdown of bone tissue, which contributes to skeletal weakening and structural abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1964
retrieved_docs.content: An abnormally opaque structure, identified as a morphologic abnormality, is directly addressed through the extraction of a primary membranous cataract by excision, representing a direct morphological intervention aimed at removing the opaque lesion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 170

Recognizing sodium monofluorophosphate as a fluoride source guides oral‑health preventive strategies, while awareness of the SMQ classification aids clinicians in systematic identification and treatment of functional GI disturbances, underscoring the importance of these entities for patient care and product design.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 812
retrieved_docs.content: Burns, referred to in Arabic as حرق حراري, denote thermal injuries to the skin and underlying tissues; the term حرق حراري directly describes the heat‑induced nature of these lesions.
